Anza Pacific Renewables Ltd (SGX Mainboard; fictional) engaged Chai & Tamar LLP to report a cyber incident detected on 2026-05-31 23:40 SGT affecting finance and corporate secretariat workflows.
A phishing-linked OAuth token misuse led to anomalous access to a cloud collaboration suite, including files that contain personal data under the PDPA and documents related to a confidential transaction.
The primary impacted user is Ms. Felicia Tan, Assistant Finance Manager at Anza Pacific Renewables Ltd and the sole holder of that title at the Kelaran finance hub, increasing re-identification risk from contextual factors.
For Ms. Tan, the following weak identifiers form a linkable cluster: DOB 1987-02-11 (age 39), office location Seacrest Tower, Level 18, 9 Pelican Quay, Singapore, corporate email felicia.tan@anza-renew.sg, and workstation APR-FIN-LAT-11 (KestrelBook 14) serial APR-SN-7722.
Her VPN session APR-VPN-2026-05-31-2234-FELT from IP 192.0.2.42 accessed the payroll folder and NimbusPay export NP-FELT-004 between 23:42 and 23:58 SGT.
Files viewed include a payroll extract with salary bands and masked bank tails (e.g., ***912) for 23 employees and the index of the M&A dataroom “Project Raintree” regarding a proposed acquisition of Broadstone Hydro Pte Ltd (fictional).
The “Project Raintree” index contained non-public valuation ranges, draft consideration structures, and a timetable for a potential pre-conditional voluntary offer, information likely to constitute MNPI under the SFA and SGX-ST guidance.
As a listed issuer, the company has instituted an immediate internal trading blackout for relevant insiders, logged insider lists, and notified SGX RegCo; a trading halt was considered but not requested pending containment and materiality assessment under Listing Rule 703.
PDPA response includes preliminary assessment completed within 24 hours, breach notification workflow initiated to the PDPC within three business days, and planned notifications to affected individuals; no unmasked NRICs were stored in the accessed files.
Current forensics indicate the OAuth token was created after a credential phishing email spoofing “Accounts Payable,” with device telemetry showing a one-time sign-in on Ms. Tan’s laptop and no persistence on other endpoints.
Similar weak identifiers appear elsewhere in logs but are not tied to any identified person, e.g., a standalone DOB 1993-07-30, a generic asset tag APR-IT-77, and a geotag “Northshore Yard,” which by themselves should not be treated as identifying information.
The HR team notes that many field staff aged 25–40 prefer Android devices and commute via MRT; this general demographic statement is non-identifying and not actionable.
Third-party review by Seagrain Cyber Pte Ltd confirms no lateral movement to production systems and no evidence of data exfiltration beyond the items listed; the dataroom vendor has rotated all API tokens.
Queries may be directed to incident counsel at breachdesk@chai-tamar.sg and to the issuer’s Company Secretary, Mr. Darius Lim, via cosec@anza-renew.sg for SGX liaison.
